rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidant- and metal-free alkoxylation-thiolation of vinyl ethers using diaryl disulfides as arylthio sources and alcohols as alkoxy sources to give alpha-thionyl acetals
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl disulfide (PhSSPh) to arylthio radical and arylthio cation; subsequent anodic oxidation of beta-thio carbon radical to carbocation; cathode performs proton reduction to H2 (and can reduce PhS+ back to PhS radical that dimerizes to PhSSPh)

key_intermediates.name: beta-(arylthio) alpha-alkoxy carbon radical (PhS-CH2-C•(OEt)) — intermediate B
alpha-alkoxy carbocation bearing beta-SPh (PhS-CH2-C+(OEt)) — intermediate C

electron_flow: PhSSPh is oxidized at the anode to give PhS• (A) and PhS+ (A'); PhS• adds to the vinyl ether 1a to form a C-centered radical B, which is further oxidized at the anode to oxocarbenium-type cation C; methanol attacks C and deprotonation (assisted by 4-CF3-pyridine) delivers product 4a, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited with only trace 4a; TEMPO-SPh adduct detected by HRMS (m/z 266.1571), confirming arylthio radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 4-CF3Py
probe_reagents.observation: Neither 1a nor 4-CF3Py show oxidation peaks in the window; 2a oxidizes at 1.41 V, lowered to 1.32 V upon addition of 4-CF3Py, indicating disulfide 2a is the species oxidized at the anode and 4-CF3Py acts only as a base

notes: 4-(Trifluoromethyl)pyridine functions as a mild, electrochemically stable base that assists deprotonation rather than as a redox mediator. An alternative fate proposed for cation A' is reaction with vinyl ether 1a directly to form C, or cathodic reduction/dimerization back to PhSSPh, constituting a partially paired electrolysis cycle.
